MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-11-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Conference
Gabriela Munoz-Gomez, Esq., Brayton Purcell 415-399-3123 gmunozgomez@braytonlaw.com Attorneys for Plaintiff, MITCHELL D. BOULWARE
Daniel S. Hurwitz, Esq. WILSON, ELSER, MOSKOWITZ, (213) 443-5100 Dan.Hurwitz@wilsonelser.com
Attorneys for Defendant, LOWE'S HOME CENTERS, LLC

Hearing is held. ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling with as follows:

DEFENDANT LOWE'S HOME CENTERS, LLC's MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT.

Defendant Lowe's Home Centers, LLC seeks an order striking from Plaintiff's complaint his statement that he may seek relief for other stone slab products that caused his injury in the course of discovery. The court denies Lowe's motion to strike as to these allegations. Plaintiff's allegations are proper under Bockrath v. Aldrich Chemical Co., Inc. (1999) 21 Cal.4th 71, 80-81, which approved similar language. Lowe's attempts to distinguish Bockrath on the grounds that that case involved manufacturers and not a retailer like Lowe's, but that distinction is immaterial for purposes of the pleading standard.

The court also denies Lowe's request to strike Plaintiff's prayer for punitive damages and related allegations. The court has sustained the demurrer to Plaintiff's fraudulent concealment cause of action in a companion order. But looking only to Plaintiff's remaining causes of action, his allegations that Lowe's and others in the countertop industry, including retailers, were aware of the hazards of their products and continued to sell them without disclosing those hazards are sufficient, at the pleading stage, to support a prayer for punitive damages as acts of implied malice or oppression in light of the harms Plaintiff has alleged from those products. (See, e.g., Pilliod v. Monsanto Company (2021) 67 Cal.App.5th 591 [punitive damages after trial].)

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling as modified in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-11-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Conference
Gabriela Munoz-Gomez, Esq., Brayton Purcell 415-399-3123 gmunozgomez@braytonlaw.com Attorneys for Plaintiff, MITCHELL D. BOULWARE
Daniel S. Hurwitz, Esq. WILSON, ELSER, MOSKOWITZ, (213) 443-5100 Dan.Hurwitz@wilsonelser.com
Attorneys for Defendant, LOWE'S HOME CENTERS, LLC

Hearing is held. ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument modifies and adopts the tentative ruling with as follows:

DEFENDANT LOWE'S HOME CENTERS, LLC's DEMURRER to COMPLAINT.

Defendant Lowe's Home Centers LLC demurs to plaintiff Mitchell D. Boulware's second, third, and fourth causes of action.

Lowe's demurrer to the second cause of action (products liability - failure to warn) and third cause of action (products liability - design defect) is overruled. Plaintiff has adequately alleged that Lowe's stores sold products that are the subject of these causes of action. (Compl. paras. 24-28, 66.) To the extent Lowe's relies on facts extrinsic to the complaint about the characteristics of the products specified in the complaint (e.g. its statement that specified products are "finished" countertops rather than stone slabs), these facts are not cognizable on demurrer, which takes the facts in the complaint as true.

(See word minutes for complete details)

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling as modified in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)
